quality.rationale: Requires full tensor kinematic decomposition derivation linking time-independent spatial metric to vanishing expansion and shear; multi-step GR formalism.

origin.question: 19.38 In cosmology one usually uses coordinates comoving with the galaxies. Let $(r, x^i)$ be such a system, and let the metric take the general form$$\mathrm {d s}^{2} = - \mathrm {d} \tau^{2} + 2 \mathrm {g}_{0 \mathrm {i}} \mathrm {d} \tau \mathrm {d x}^{\mathrm {i}} + \mathrm {g}_{\mathrm {i j}} \mathrm {d x}^{\mathrm {i}} \mathrm {d x}^{\mathrm {j}}$$where $\mathbf{g}_{0\mathrm{i}}$ and $\mathbf{g}_{\mathrm{ij}}$ can be functions of $\pmb{\tau}$ and $\mathbf{x}^{\mathrm{i}}$ . Show (d) that if $\mathbf{g}_{0\mathrm{i}}$ and $\mathbf{g}_{\mathrm{ij}}$ are independent of $\pmb{\tau}$ , then $\sigma_{\alpha \beta} = 0$ , $\theta = 0$ (but $\omega_{\alpha \beta} \neq 0$ in general);
